Maintainers will use it to drive their review.
-->

## Summary

<!-- One paragraph: what does this PR change and why? -->

## Linked issues

<!-- Use `Fixes #123` / `Closes #456` / `Refs #789` so they auto-link and close. -->

- Fixes #
- Closes #
- Refs #

## Type of change

<!-- Check one — delete the rest. -->

- [ ] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)
- [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change that adds functionality)
- [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would change existing behavior)
- [ ] 📝 Documentation / docs only
- [ ] 🧹 Chore / refactor (no functional change)
- [ ] ⬆️ Dependency upgrade (Dependabot, etc.)
- [ ] 🔒 Security fix

## Scope

<!-- What part of the codebase? Be specific. -->

- [ ] `src/` frontend
- [ ] `src-tauri/` Rust backend
- [ ] `docs/`
- [ ] `.github/` workflows / templates
- [ ] `docker/`
- [ ] `skills/`
- [ ] Other (describe below)

## How has this been tested?

<!-- Be specific: commands run, screenshots, manual steps. -->

- [ ] Unit tests: `bun run test`
- [ ] TypeScript: `bun run check` (`tsc --noEmit`)
- [ ] Lint: `bun run lint` (if configured)
- [ ] Manual dev: `bun run dev` + `bun run tauri dev`
- [ ] Production build: `bun run build` (or `bun run tauri build`)
- [ ] Manual reproduction steps:

  ```
  1.
  2.
  3.
  ```

## Checklist

- [ ] My code follows the project's [CONTRIBUTING.md](../../CONTRIBUTING.md) style.
- [ ] I have added tests that prove my fix / feature works.
- [ ] New and existing unit tests pass locally (`bun run test`).
- [ ] I have updated relevant documentation (`README.md`, `docs/`, `CHANGELOG.md`).
- [ ] I have added an entry to `CHANGELOG.md` under `[Unreleased]` (or the version being released).
- [ ] I have **not** committed any secrets, `.env` files, or generated build artifacts (`dist/`, `target/`).
- [ ] I have rebased on the latest `main` (or the target branch).
- [ ] I have read and agree to follow [CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md](../../CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) and [SECURITY.md](../../SECURITY.md).
